title: Customer Support Triage Bot For A Small SaaS Startup
description: A guarded support architecture that redacts PII, retrieves approved policy, checks compliance, and escalates sensitive cases.
user_idea: Build a customer support bot for my startup that answers FAQs, checks policy, and escalates billing or angry customer messages.
assumptions: Customer messages may contain personal or billing information.
The bot should draft responses but avoid account changes.
Billing, legal, security, and angry customer cases require escalation.

naive_baseline.summary: Customer messages go straight to an LLM, which answers without redaction, approved policy retrieval, permission gating, or escalation.
failure_modes: The response may contradict refund or billing policy.
Sensitive customer data can be exposed in prompts or outputs.
The bot may attempt an account action without permission.

nodes.name: Customer Message

risks.description: Customer messages can include personal or billing details.
risks.mitigation: Redact PII before routing to other components.

alternatives.name: Structured ticket form
alternatives.tradeoff: Cleaner data but more friction for customers.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: This is where the customer's request enters the support system.

nodes.name: PII Redaction
nodes.description: Removes or masks personal data before classification and response drafting.

risks.description: PII redaction may miss unusual personal data.
risks.mitigation: Combine redaction with reviewer escalation for sensitive topics.

cost_estimate.notes: Can use rules or a small classifier.

latency_estimate.notes: Should be fast enough for chat.

alternatives.name: Manual redaction
alternatives.tradeoff: Higher accuracy for rare cases but slower.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: Redaction reduces the chance that private customer data spreads through the system.

nodes.name: Intent Classifier
nodes.description: Classifies the message as FAQ, billing, angry customer, bug report, security, or legal.

risks.description: Misclassified billing or legal cases could get an unsafe response.

alternatives.name: Rule-first routing
alternatives.tradeoff: Predictable for obvious cases but brittle.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: The classifier decides whether the bot can help or should involve a person.

nodes.name: FAQ And Policy Knowledge Base
nodes.description: Stores approved FAQ, refund, billing, and support policy content.

outputs.name: approved_policy
outputs.description: Searchable support policy and FAQ entries.

risks.description: Outdated policy can cause wrong customer commitments.
risks.mitigation: Show policy version and review cadence in exported plan.

alternatives.name: Static FAQ only
alternatives.tradeoff: Simpler but misses nuanced policy cases.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: This is the approved source of truth for customer answers.

nodes.name: Retrieval
nodes.description: Finds relevant FAQ or policy passages for the customer message.

outputs.name: policy_context
outputs.description: Relevant policy passages with source labels.

config.fallback_when_empty: escalate or say policy could not be found

risks.description: If policy is not found, the bot may guess.
risks.mitigation: Fallback to escalation when retrieval confidence is low.

alternatives.name: Direct policy lookup by intent
alternatives.tradeoff: Simpler for common intents but less flexible.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: Retrieval gives the model the approved policy text before it drafts anything.

nodes.name: Response Draft LLM
nodes.description: Drafts a customer response from the redacted message and retrieved policy.

risks.description: The model may promise refunds or actions that policy does not allow.
risks.mitigation: Check draft with policy compliance evaluator before final response.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: The LLM writes a draft, but only after receiving the approved policy context.

nodes.name: Policy Compliance Evaluator
nodes.description: Checks whether the draft response follows approved policy and avoids unsafe commitments.

risks.description: The evaluator may fail to catch every policy issue.
risks.mitigation: Escalate sensitive intents and low-confidence reviews.

cost_estimate.notes: May require a second model call.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: This checkpoint asks whether the draft is allowed by the company's policy.

nodes.name: Tool Permission Gate
nodes.description: Prevents account changes and sensitive actions unless a human explicitly approves them.

outputs.name: allowed_action
outputs.description: Safe action decision or escalation request.

risks.description: Support tools can affect customer accounts or billing.
risks.mitigation: Keep this gate human-approved for sensitive actions.

alternatives.name: No tool access
alternatives.tradeoff: Safer for MVP but less useful for support operations.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: This gate stops the bot from taking actions it should not take on its own.

nodes.name: Human Escalation
nodes.description: Routes sensitive, angry, legal, security, billing, or low-confidence cases to a person.

outputs.name: approved_response
outputs.description: Response approved or rewritten by support staff.

risks.mitigation: Use clear triage labels and draft context to speed review.

cost_estimate.notes: Human time cost, not model cost.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: A human handles cases where the bot should not be the final decision maker.

nodes.name: Final Response
nodes.description: Sends or displays the approved support response with escalation status.

risks.description: Customers may not know when a case is escalated.
risks.mitigation: State clearly when a human will follow up.

alternatives.name: Internal draft only
alternatives.tradeoff: Safer for early MVP but less automated.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: This is the safe response that reaches the customer after checks and escalation.

edges.label: sensitive or low-confidence intent
edges.condition: billing, legal, security, angry customer, or low confidence
